product.name: ArWoWear T20 Flame Pro
product.categorySourceGroup: Flame Retardant, Vat Dyed, Workwear Fabrics Resistant up to 100 Wash
product.titleDescriptor: 80% Cotton 20% Polyester, Flame Retardant Fabric , 260 gr/m'2
product.specLine: 260 g/m², 80% Cotton 20% Polyester, VAT Dyed, Flame Retardant (100 Wash Resistant), 3/1S Twill Fabric
descriptions: ArWoWear T20 Flame Pro is light weight 260 g/m², 80% cotton 20% polyester, vat dyed, flame retardant workwear fabric.
It has flame retardant function with EN ISO 11612 and EN ISO 11611 certification.
It is durable up to 100 washing.
80% Cotton 20% Polyester, Flame Retardant Fabric , 260 gr/m'2
260 g/m², 80% Cotton 20% Polyester, VAT Dyed, Flame Retardant (100 Wash Resistant), 3/1S Twill Fabric
technicalParameters: Tensile Strength | Warp>1000N; Weft>800N | EN ISO 13934-1
Tear Strength | Warp>20N; Weft>15N | EN ISO 13937-2
Dimensional Change | -3% < Length < +3% ; -3% < Width < +3% | EN ISO 5077
Dry Cleaning Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 D01
Hot Press Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 X11
Perspiration Light Fast. | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 B07
Azo Test | No Azo Colorants. | EN ISO 14362-1
Flame and Heat Protection | Yes | EN ISO 11612

acceptedMatches.rationale: Explicit EN ISO 11611 welding protection certification and molten-metal splash resistance directly indicate welding/metalworking use.

possibleButNotAccepted.reason: Explicit electric-arc protection rating (EN 61482, ATPV) directly indicates suitability for electrical/high-voltage work environments.
EN ISO 11612 flame/heat protection is present but this is generic FR workwear certification, not explicit firefighter turnout gear use.

primary.rationale: Explicit EN ISO 11611 welding protection certification plus molten-metal splash resistance directly indicate welding/metalworking protective clothing use, making this the most specific named sector.

secondary.rationale: The fabric is explicitly positioned as general FR workwear/coveralls with broad protective certification (EN ISO 11612), supporting a secondary general industrial workwear use beyond the narrower welding application.

alternativesConsidered.reasonNotSelected: Electric-arc protection (EN 61482) is present but is a property/hazard rating rather than an explicit statement of electrical utility/high-voltage sector use.
EN ISO 11612 is generic flame/heat protection certification, not explicit firefighter turnout gear use.
